metadata.title: Overview of Comments Received on Reflection Paper on Regulatory Requirements for the Development of Medicinal Products for Chronic Non-Infectious Liver Diseases (PBC, PSC, NASH)
metadata.notes: Rules extracted exclusively from the Outcome (CHMP position) column. Stakeholder comments are not included. Document is a public consultation overview with 194 pages covering NASH, PBC, and PSC development requirements.

rules.rule_text: For a rare disease such as PSC, regulatory approval will depend on the totality of data supporting safety and efficacy. An effect on histology alone, without an effect on cholestatic markers, would not be considered clinically meaningful.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is agreed that for a rare disease as PSC, evidence will depend on the totality of data. It is not agreed that an effect on histology alone, without an effect on cholestatic markers, would be clinically meaningful.

rules.rule_text: Screening for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) using faecal calprotectin (fCP) is recommended in clinical practice for paediatric PSC patients. Routine screening for cholangiocarcinoma (CCA) is not indicated for paediatric PSC patients given the very rare incidence of CCA in this population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed - For paediatric age CCA is very rare condition and screening is not indicated. Screening for IBD using fCP is recommended in clinical practice.

rules.rule_text: The reflection paper on regulatory requirements for chronic non-infectious liver diseases shall be split into two separate documents: one reflection paper covering PBC and PSC (cholestatic liver diseases), and a separate reflection paper on NASH. NASH is considered a different type of disease requiring its own document.
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed. There will be two reflection papers (one on NASH and another one on PBC and PSC).

rules.rule_text: The Reflection paper constitutes a preliminary regulatory position statement only, applicable to situations where regulatory licensing experience in the intended clinical indication is limited or non-existent. Only general recommendations on specific study endpoints are given, as there is no clear accepted full spectrum of methods.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partially agreed. However, since there is no clear accepted full spectrum of methods, only general recommendations are given. It should also be noted that a reflection paper is a preliminary regulatory position statement only in a situation when regulatory experience with licensing in the intended clinical indication is limited (or non-existing).

rules.rule_text: Fatigue and pruritus are agreed to be important symptoms in PBC and PSC clinical trials. Symptom evaluation shall be part of any trial in PBC and PSC. Development of patient reported outcome (PRO) tools for symptom evaluation is encouraged. No fully validated scales are currently available, but partly validated scales can be included in trials for both evaluation and validation purposes.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is agreed that fatigue and pruritus are important symptoms. The Reflection paper states that symptom evaluation should be part of any trial in PBC and PSC. Furthermore, the paper has now added a separate section on drug development for the indication of cholestatic pruritus specifically as separate from disease modification. Partly agreed. Recommendations are limited due to the fact that currently no fully validated scales exist. Development of patient reported outcome tools is encouraged.
exceptions: Partly validated scales are acceptable for inclusion purposes

rules.rule_text: Bilirubin levels should be defined for inclusion criteria in clinical trials with disease modification objectives. Bilirubin is also recommended to be considered for enrichment of patients in trials with the objective of disease modification. Total bilirubin (not conjugated bilirubin) is the standard measurement used in drug development, as used in defining Hy's Law and in the MELD score.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — General Comments (Stakeholder 9) / Section 2 — Specific Comments (Stakeholder 9)
rules.verbatim_quote: Partly agreed. Levels of bilirubin for inclusion should be defined. Bilirubin is also recommended to be considered for enrichment of patients in trials with the objective of disease modification. Not agreed. Total bilirubin is most commonly measured as standard in drug development and used for example in defining Hy's Law and in the MELD score.

rules.rule_text: A responder analysis is recommended in the PBC/PSC Reflection paper for endpoints. Responder analyses (e.g. proportions of patients with particular decreases in ALP levels or fatigue scores) provide insight into the extent of treatment efficacy.
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed. A responder analysis is recommended in the Reflection paper.

rules.rule_text: In clinical trials for NASH, liver biopsies are required in cases where interim evaluation and licensing via Conditional Marketing Authorisation (CMA) is intended. All cases that could proceed without histology are discussed adequately in the Reflection paper. There is a clear need for liver biopsies for interim evaluation in the NASH clinical trial setting.
rules.verbatim_quote: The comment is noted, but not agreed with. In the clinical trial setting, there is a clear need for liver biopsies in cases where interim evaluation and licensing via CMA is intended. All cases which could go without histology are discussed adequately.
exceptions: Exploratory clinical trials (Phase 1 and early Phase 2) may use non-invasive methods if based on sound scientific principles

rules.rule_text: For subjects with fibrosis stage 4 (F4) compensated cirrhosis, a paragraph on development and endpoints in compensated cirrhosis has been included in the NASH Reflection paper. Reversal of fibrosis (reversal of cirrhosis) is included as a potential surrogate endpoint for trials in cirrhotic NASH patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed. A respective paragraph on development/endpoints in compensated cirrhosis has been included. Agreed Reversal of cirrrhosis is included as potentail surrogate for trials in cirrhotic patients.

rules.rule_text: PBC does not occur in children; therefore a waiver for a paediatric programme is accepted for medicinal products being developed for PBC. PSC is relevant for children. For paediatric NASH, non-pharmacological interventions are the preferential approach. Validation of non-invasive methods in children, development of age-appropriate formulations and dose finding are priorities for paediatric NASH development.
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed and reflected in GL waiver is accepted. Agreed, noted. Validation of non-invasive methods in children in addition to biopsy/histology, development of age appropriate formulations and dose finding are priorities.
exceptions: PSC is not subject to the paediatric waiver; clinical trials in paediatric PSC can reasonably be undertaken

rules.rule_text: For paediatric NASH diagnosis and efficacy evaluation, diagnosis is based on histology and efficacy of treatment is evaluated by repeated biopsies. Since no validated non-invasive tests/markers are available for children, histology is required. Histological evaluation differs between adults and paediatric patients and a different histological scoring system needs to be validated for the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed, partially reflected in GL. Diagnosis is based on histology and efficacy of treatment is evaluated by repeated biopsies. Since we don't have validated non-invasive test/markers, we need to rely on histology. Histological evaluation differs from adults and paediatric scoring system need to be validated.
cross_references: EMA/199678/2016 — Reflection paper on extrapolation in paediatrics

rules.rule_text: Based on stakeholder comments, it was decided to split the original single Reflection paper into a reflection paper on PBC/PSC (cholestatic liver diseases) and a separate reflection paper on NASH. Proposed textual adjustments from stakeholders were considered for each of the new, separated reflection papers.
rules.verbatim_quote: Based on stakeholders' comments it was decided to split the original Reflection paper into a reflection paper on PBC/PSC, and another reflection paper on NASH. The proposed textual adjustments were considered for each of the new, separated reflection papers.

rules.rule_text: The use of non-invasive methods is currently recommended for enrichment purposes only in NASH clinical trials. Conditions for use of non-invasive methods (imaging, biomarkers, or combinations) in early exploratory clinical trials are permissible if based on sound scientific principles for which uncertainties can be quantified and later stage trials planned accordingly. Histology is required for later-phase (Phase 2b and Phase 3) trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comments noted. Currently, the use of non-invasive methods is recommended for enrichment purposes only. Conditions for use of non-invasive methods in early trials are included in Chapter 5.2.2. Partially agreed. See above. The text now reads as follows: 'Deviations for exploratory clinical trials, e.g. using imaging methods, or biomarkers (or a combination of those) only, are possible if based on sound scientific principles, for which the uncertainties can be quantified and later stage trials be planned accordingly.'
exceptions: Exploratory trials (Phase 1 and early Phase 2) may deviate from histology requirement if scientifically justified

rules.rule_text: For inclusion of patients in clinical trials of non-cirrhotic NASH, histological confirmation of NASH is required. The baseline biopsy should be no more than 6 months before enrollment. A NAS (NAFLD Activity Score) of 4 or higher is the accepted inclusion criterion, implemented in multiple clinical trials. A certain level of disease activity (inflammation, ballooning, and fat accumulation) should be present in the trial population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partially agreed. Wording changed due to new structure. Requirement for historical biopsy has been set to 6 months. The criteria for inclusion based on NAS of 4 or higher have been accepted and implemented in multiple clinical trials. Not agreed. A certain level of disease activity in all the features assures adequacy of treatment need, and the sensitivity to change.
exceptions: For early phase 2 (exploratory) trials, a selection of patients on the basis of a known histological diagnosis of NASH or a combination of biochemical criteria and/or imaging evidence of steatosis/steatohepatitis/fibrosis may be appropriate

rules.rule_text: Fibrosis stage 1 (F1) patients are currently only recommended for inclusion in therapeutic trials in NASH for exploratory purposes. Identification of 'fast progressors' among F1 patients remains an evolving field and no clear recommendation for their specific inclusion in therapeutic trials is possible at this time.
rules.verbatim_quote: Not agreed. Identification of 'fast progressors' is still regard and 'evolving' field on which no clear recommendation seems possible. Not agreed. The sentence is left unchanged. The purpose of including these patients is left to the discretion of the Sponsors.
exceptions: Inclusion of F1 patients is at Sponsor's discretion for exploratory purposes

rules.rule_text: For NASH patients with decompensated cirrhosis, no clear recommendations for development programmes are given and the section is kept unspecific, given that experience with development programmes in this population is very limited.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comment noted. The paragraph on decompensated cirrhosis is kept unspecific only and no clear recommendations given. Experience with development programmes in this population is very limited.

rules.rule_text: For NASH with compensated NASH-cirrhosis (F4), the requirement for 'burnt-out NASH' diagnosis requires rather strict criteria to assure that only true NASH-cirrhosis patients are admitted to trials. The primary aim of strict criteria is to assure diagnostic accuracy. Reference is made to the possibility of proposing alternative criteria within a Scientific Advice procedure, once accurate data can be presented for less stringent criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: Not agreed. Diagnostic criteria for T2DM are not needed in a paper on NASH. The requirements implemented for the 'burnt-out NASH' population have been amended. Rather strict criteria are used, since it the 'diagnostic accuracy' of the Liver Forum classification is unclear (See Chapter 5.24). The primary aim of the proposed criteria was to assure that only 'true NASH-cirrhosis' patients are admitted to the trials.
exceptions: Alternative less stringent criteria may be proposed via Scientific Advice

rules.rule_text: The acceptable intermediate endpoints for NASH pivotal trials consist of two co-primary composite endpoints evaluated at the individual patient level: (1) resolution of NASH with no worsening of the stage of fibrosis; AND (2) improvement of fibrosis by at least 1 stage without any worsening of NASH. The two-stage criterion for fibrosis improvement at interim has been deleted; however both outcomes remain relevant. If only one endpoint is met, results must be convincing in all other aspects.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partially agreed. The two-stage criterion has been deleted. However, both outcomes are considered relevant. In case only one can be met, the results would need to be convincing in all other aspects in order to overcome the fact that: In case fibrosis improvement is met only, the ongoing insult (liver cell stress with ballooning and inflammation, as well as steatosis) may outweigh the short-term benefits in the long run. In case the resolution of NASH endpoint is met only, a missing effect on fibrosis may not indicate improvement of prognosis.
exceptions: For anti-fibrotic mechanism of action drugs, Scientific Advice is recommended if co-primary endpoints cannot both be met
cross_references: EMA/CHMP/505049/2015 — Reflection paper on cardiovascular safety

rules.rule_text: The duration of NASH clinical trials should be of sufficient duration depending on phase 2 results and other factors. The revised wording opens requirements for duration depending on phase 2 results. A more flexible approach is given; nevertheless, 2 years is still given as the recommended timeframe for interim evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partially agreed. The revised wording opens the requirements for the duration depending on phase 2 results and other factors. Partially agreed. A more flexible approach is given, nevertheless, the 2-years are still given as recommendation.
exceptions: Duration may vary based on phase 2 results, mechanism of action, and statistical requirements

rules.rule_text: The need for dedicated cardiovascular outcome trials is a case-by-case decision in NASH drug development, to be decided upon based on pre-clinical and clinical safety data. The existing reflection paper on assessment of cardiovascular safety profile of medicinal products (EMA/CHMP/505049/2015) provides the relevant framework, and there is no need to address cardiovascular outcome trial requirements in further detail in the NASH reflection paper.
rules.verbatim_quote: Not agreed. Since the paper refers to the 'reflection paper on assessment of cardiovascular safety profile of medicinal products' (EMA/CHMP/505049/2015) there is no need to fix the details further. The need for CV outcome studies is a case by case decision, and has to be decided upon based on pre-clinical and clinical safety data.

rules.rule_text: Currently there is no finally validated, regulatorily approved non-invasive biomarker available to replace liver biopsy for NASH diagnosis. The final aim of developing reliable, fully validated biomarkers potentially replacing histology is expressed in the NASH reflection paper.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comment noted. The final aim of developing reliable, fully validated biomarkers potentially replacing histology is also expressed in the Nash reflection paper. Comment noted Currently, there is no finally validated, regulatorily approved biomarker available.

rules.rule_text: The long-term outcome endpoint for demonstration of efficacy in NASH is a composite endpoint with the components: all-cause death, decompensation of liver disease (hepatic encephalopathy, variceal bleeding, ascites), liver transplantation, and (histological) diagnosis of liver cirrhosis. The MELD threshold is set at similar or higher than 15 (MELD ≥15), based on the EASL and AASLD guidelines on liver transplantation. Cirrhosis is accepted as part of the final composite in non-cirrhotic NASH.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comment and proposal noted. Bullet list not implemented. MELD score >15 not agreed. the current similar or higher than 15 MELD criterion is based on the EASL as well ass AASLD guidelines on liver transplantation. Agreed. Liver transplantation is mentioned. Partially agreed. However, cirrhosis as such is not a 'hard' endpoint in the strict sense, and there is wide variability in severity of liver disease in different cirrhosis patients. Nevertheless, cirrhosis is accepted as part of the final commposite in non-cirrrhotic NASH.

rules.rule_text: HCC (hepatocellular carcinoma) is dealt with in a separate paragraph in the NASH reflection paper and its role as a long-term outcome endpoint component is addressed. The three acceptable elements of decompensation are mentioned in the Reflection paper, and e.g. HCC (and other potential events) are dealt with in Chapter 5.3.4.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comment noted. HCC has been dealt with in a separate paragraph. Comment noted. The three 'acceptable' elements of decompensation are mentioned, and e.g. HCC (and other potential events) are dealt with in Chapter 5.3.4.

rules.rule_text: For NASH combination therapy development, there is no clear restriction to second-line usage only. The section on combination treatment has been revised. Clinical utility shall determine the stage of clinical use. The previous absolute requirement for second-line or high-risk patients treatment has been largely abandoned.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partially agreed. There is no clear restriction to second line any more. The section on combination treatment has been revised. The comments are partially agreed, and the previous absolute requirement for second line or high risk patients treatment has been largely abandoned.

rules.rule_text: For NASH development, the focus of treatment will remain on the liver. Cardiovascular disease is included in the composite final endpoint evaluation and is dealt with as a main safety aspect (see NASH reflection paper chapter 5.3.7.2). Treatments for NASH are or should be primarily intended to treat liver disease.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comment noted and partially agreed. However, the focuse of treatment of NASH will remain on the liver. Cardiovascular disease will be included in the composite final endpoint evaluation, and has to be dealt with as a main safety aspect (see chapter 5.3.7.2).

rules.rule_text: The requirement for patients to have undertaken at least one unsuccessful attempt with a weight-reducing diet before inclusion in NASH clinical trials has been deleted. Chapter 5.2.1 of the NASH reflection paper includes only the requirement for stable weight before inclusion.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comment noted and partially agreed. The paragraph with regard to attempts to lose weight has been deleted. Chapter 5.2.1 only includes the requirement for stable weight before inclusion.

rules.rule_text: For PBC patient selection in clinical trials, the requirement for an elevated bilirubin (bilirubin >1xULN) at baseline has been removed. Inclusion criteria have been made more flexible. Inclusion of at least a subgroup of patients with baseline histology evaluation is still recommended for PBC trials.
rules.source_section: Section 2 — Specific Comments, PBC Section 4.3.2, Lines 423-431 / Lines 427-428
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed. The requirement for a bilirubin elevation baseline has been removed. Partly agreed. The requirement for an elevated bilirubin has been removed and inclusion criteria have been made more flexible. Partly agreed. Inclusion of at least a subgroup of patients with baseline histology evaluation is, however, still recommended.

rules.rule_text: For PBC trials, the medical need for a second-line treatment is stated in the Reflection paper. The ALP ≥2xULN criterion combined with elevated risk indicators after adequate UDCA therapy remains the recommended patient selection approach. Additional criteria with regard to transaminases, albumin, GGT, or risk scores (Globe or UKPBC-Mayo) may be applied if adequately justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partly agreed. The medical need for a second-line treatment is stated. Partly agreed. The inclusion criteria have been made more flexible. The role of risk calculators is not established in the clinical trial setting.
exceptions: Additional criteria (transaminases, albumin, GGT, Globe score, UKPBC-Mayo score) may be applied if adequately justified

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials in paediatric PSC can reasonably be undertaken since a relevant amount of data on natural history has already been collected. Inclusion of patients should be based on risk factors identified for paediatric PSC, which are distinct from adult PSC, such as elevated GGT and aspartate aminotransferase-to-platelet ratio index (at diagnosis). Patients with AIH-PSC overlap conditions may be included. Recommendations for trial design and endpoints in paediatric PSC are not expected to be very different from those in adult patients. Consultation with the agency early in drug development (scientific advice and PIP submission) is therefore advisable.
rules.verbatim_quote: Agreed and reflected in relevant paragraph. No update needed.

rules.rule_text: For NASH clinical trial missing data strategy, the estimand strategy description has been revised. The recommendation relates to an adequate missing data strategy and the presentation of sensitivity analyses. A responder analysis (binary classification) is recommended as the primary analysis approach, given the inherent limitations of the biopsy-based endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partially agreed. Estimand strategy description has been revised. Recommendation however, relates to an adequate missing data strategy, and presentation of sensitivity analyses.
